name: Linda Ronstadt
abstract: Linda Maria Ronstadt (born July 15, 1946) is a retired American singer and musician who performed and recorded in diverse genres including rock, folk, pop, country, and soul. Ronstadt has earned 11 Grammy Awards, three American Music Awards, two Academy of Country Music awards, an Emmy Award, and an ALMA Award.

Rosemary Clooney and The Arrangers / A Tribute to Nelson Riddle
Carnegie Hall Celebrates The Music of Frank Sinatra

about_quotes.speaker: Josiah Gluck
about_quotes.evidence: there was a hit a number of years ago with Aaron Neville and Linda Ronstadt, “Don’t Know Much.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Josiah Gluck interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 13, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: He was a producer too, you know. Produced some records with Linda Ronstadt.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: So as an acoustic bassist I’ve gotten a chance to record a lot of pop music, people like Rod Stewart and George Michael and Linda Ronstadt and others like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker
about_quotes.evidence: there were a lot of people like Leo Sayer or Eric Clapton or Elvis Costello or Linda Ronstadt... where they didn’t have a band so they used a put together band from New York or they used certain members of the Saturday Night band itself to do their performance.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chris Parker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 5, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Magnusson
about_quotes.evidence: Bob has recorded and performed with a long list of major artists including Sarah Vaughn, Art Pepper, Linda Ronstadt and Roger Kellaway.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Magnusson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: when she decided she wanted to make an album of her father’s stuff ... I got the call. And that’s what we did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
